questions.question: (a) Derive the Boolean expressions for Sum (S) and Carry (C) outputs of a Full Adder. (b) Draw the truth table for a Full Adder with inputs A, B, and Cin. (c) Implement the Full Adder circuit using one Half Adder and one OR gate, showing proper connections.
keyPoints: Sum = A ⊕ B ⊕ Cin, Carry = (A·B) + (Cin·(A ⊕ B))
Truth table: 8 combinations showing all input cases
First Half Adder adds A and B, second Half Adder adds result with Cin
OR gate combines carries from both Half Adders

questions.question: Perform the following binary subtractions using 2's complement method: (i) 11001 - 10011 (ii) 10010 - 11011. Show all steps including 2's complement conversion and indicate whether overflow occurs in each case. Also explain the condition for overflow in signed binary subtraction.
keyPoints: 2's complement obtained by inverting bits and adding 1
Add the 2's complement of subtrahend to minuend
Discard end-around carry if present (indicates no overflow)
Overflow occurs when signs of operands differ and sign of result differs from sign of minuend

questions.question: Design a 4-bit Binary Adder-Subtractor circuit using full adders and explain its working with the control input M. (a) Show the circuit diagram with proper labeling of all components. (b) When M=0, the circuit performs addition. When M=1, it performs subtraction (A - B) using 2's complement. Explain the operation with Boolean expressions for each stage. (c) Add overflow detection logic to the circuit and explain how it works for both signed and unsigned numbers. (d) Demonstrate the circuit operation for: (i) A=0110, B=0011 with M=0 (ii) A=1010, B=0101 with M=1.
keyPoints: Each full adder XOR input B with M: B ⊕ M acts as B for M=0 and B' for M=1
2's complement achieved by XORing B with 1 (M=1) and adding 1 through initial carry
Overflow detection: V = Cn ⊕ Cn-1 (XOR of final two carries) for signed arithmetic
For unsigned: overflow indicated by final carry out; for signed: use V = Cn ⊕ Cn-1

questions.question: (a) Design a 4-bit Parallel Adder using ripple carry architecture. Explain the propagation delay issue and derive the total propagation delay expression. (b) Solve using the above adder: Add the signed numbers (represented in 2's complement with 8 bits): X = 00110110 and Y = 11011001. Show step-by-step addition, final binary result, and determine if overflow occurred. (c) Explain the difference between ripple carry adder and carry look-ahead adder with respect to speed improvement and hardware complexity. (d) Design the carry look-ahead generator circuit for 4 bits and show how it generates carry signals simultaneously.
KeyPoints: Propagation delay = n × (propagation delay of one full adder), where n is number of bits
For signed 2's complement: overflow V = C4 ⊕ C3; result 11110011 = -13 in decimal
Carry look-ahead reduces delay by generating carries in parallel using Generate (Gi) and Propagate (Pi) signals: Gi = Ai·Bi, Pi = Ai ⊕ Bi
Carry look-ahead: Ci+1 = Gi + Pi·Ci, allowing parallel computation of all carries
